steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage ends with "the results of the meta-analysis suggest that ______" — we need a conclusion that follows logically from the stated evidence. The key facts: (1) mammal studies showed larger differences on average than bird studies, but (2) for every class of animals, there were individual studies showing differences well above the mammal average.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — the studies in the meta-analysis that examined mammals were more likely than those that examined birds to specify whether the observed effects were detrimental.: The passage says nothing about whether effects were labeled detrimental. This is unsupported. ✗
  • Choice B — the differences that studies attribute to exposure to anthropogenic noise are likely to be more pronounced for birds than they are for mammals.: This reverses the stated comparison — mammals averaged larger differences. ✗
  • Choice C — the difference found in the study conducted by Amy Morris-Drake and colleagues was likely larger than the average difference for studies of dwarf mongooses included in the meta-analysis.: The meta-analysis says every class had individual studies above the mammal average, but the passage gives no information about the Morris-Drake study's position relative to any average. ✗
  • Choice D — some studies of birds found larger effects of exposure to anthropogenic noise than some studies of mammals did.: If bird studies reached differences well above the mammal average, and the mammal average sits above some mammal studies, then those bird studies exceeded some mammal studies. This follows directly. ✓
That matches Choice D — some studies of birds found larger effects of exposure to anthropogenic noise than some studies of mammals did..
